name: adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx

metadata.name: adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx
metadata.summary: CircuitPython library for controlling a MCP23008 or MCP23017 I2C GPIO expander.

documentation:
# Introduction

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/mcp230xx/en/latest/
```

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_Bundle/main/badges/adafruit_discord.svg
:alt: Discord
:target: https://adafru.it/discord
```

```{image} https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_MCP230xx/workflows/Build%20CI/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_MCP230xx/actions/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:alt: 'Code Style: Black'
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

CircuitPython module for the MCP23017/08 I2C and MCP23S17/08 SPI I/O extenders.

# Dependencies

This driver depends on:

- [Adafruit CircuitPython](https://github.com/adafruit/circuitpython)
- [Bus Device](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_BusDevice)

Please ensure all dependencies are available on the CircuitPython filesystem.
This is easily achieved by downloading
[the Adafruit library and driver bundle](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_Bundle).

# Installing from PyPI

On supported GNU/Linux systems like the Raspberry Pi, you can install the driver locally [from
PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx/). To install for current user:

```shell
pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx
```

To install system-wide (this may be required in some cases):

```shell
sudo pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx
```

To install in a virtual environment in your current project:

```shell
mkdir project-name && cd project-name
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-mcp230xx
```

# Usage Example

See examples/mcp230xx_simpletest.py for a demo of the usage.

# Documentation

API documentation for this library can be found on [Read the Docs](https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/mcp230xx/en/latest/).

For information on building library documentation, please check out [this guide](https://learn.adafruit.com/creating-and-sharing-a-circuitpython-library/sharing-our-docs-on-readthedocs#sphinx-5-1).

# Warning

Some people have reported an undocumented bug that can potentially corrupt the I2C bus.
It occurs if an MCP230XX input pin state changes during I2C readout. **This should be very rare.** For more information, see this [forum post](https://www.microchip.com/forums/m646539.aspx) and this [knowledge base article](https://microchipsupport.force.com/s/article/On-MCP23008-MCP23017-SDA-line-change-when-GPIO7-input-change) .

# Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_MCP230xx/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
before contributing to help this project stay welcoming.

modules.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016
documentation:
`mcp23016`
====================================================

CircuitPython module for the MCP23016 I2C I/O extenders.

* Author(s): Diego Elio Pettenò (based on MCP23017.py)

Notes
-----

While the datasheet refers to the two 8-bit ports as port 0 and 1,
for API compatibility with more recent expanders, these are exposed as
ports A and B.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2017 Tony DiCola for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2019 Carter Nelson
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2020 Facebook Inc.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.gpio
documentation:
The raw GPIO output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.gpioa
documentation:
The raw GPIO 0 output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.gpiob
documentation:
The raw GPIO 1 output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.iodir
documentation:
The raw IODIR direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.iodira
documentation:
The raw IODIR0 direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.iodirb
documentation:
The raw IODIR0 direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.get_pin
documentation:
Convenience function to create an instance of the DigitalInOut class
pointing at the specified pin of this MCP23016 device.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.clear_inta
documentation: Clears port 0 interrupts.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23016.MCP23016.clear_intb
documentation: Clears port 1 interrupts.

Supports MCP23016 instance on specified I2C bus and optionally
at the specified I2C address.

modules.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout
documentation:
`digital_inout`
====================================================

Digital input/output of the MCP230xx.

* Author(s): Tony DiCola
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2017 Tony DiCola for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2019 Carter Nelson
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout._get_bit
documentation: Internal helpers to simplify setting and getting a bit inside an integer.

classes.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.__init__
documentation:
Specify the pin number of the MCP230xx (0...7 for MCP23008, or 0...15
for MCP23017) and MCP23008 instance.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.switch_to_output
documentation:
Switch the pin state to a digital output with the provided starting
value (True/False for high or low, default is False/low).
kwargs in switch functions below are _necessary_ for compatibility
with DigitalInout class (which allows specifying pull, etc. which
is unused by this class).  Do not remove them, instead turn off pylint
in this case.
pylint: disable=unused-argument

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.switch_to_input
documentation:
Switch the pin state to a digital input with the provided starting
pull-up resistor state (optional, no pull-up by default) and input polarity.  Note that
pull-down resistors are NOT supported!

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.value
documentation:
The value of the pin, either True for high or False for
low.  Note you must configure as an output or input appropriately
before reading and writing this value.
pylint: enable=unused-argument

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.direction
documentation:
The direction of the pin, either True for an input or
False for an output.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.pull
documentation:
Enable or disable internal pull-up resistors for this pin.  A
value of digitalio.Pull.UP will enable a pull-up resistor, and None will
disable it.  Pull-down resistors are NOT supported!

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.digital_inout.DigitalInOut.invert_polarity
documentation:
The polarity of the pin, either True for an Inverted or
False for an normal.

Digital input/output of the MCP230xx.  The interface is exactly the
same as the digitalio.DigitalInOut class, however:

  * MCP230xx family does not support pull-down resistors;
  * MCP23016 does not support pull-up resistors.

Exceptions will be thrown when attempting to set unsupported pull
configurations.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gpio
documentation:
The raw GPIO output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gpioa
documentation:
The raw GPIO A output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gpiob
documentation:
The raw GPIO B output register.  Each bit represents the
output value of the associated pin (0 = low, 1 = high), assuming that
pin has been configured as an output previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.iodir
documentation:
The raw IODIR direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.iodira
documentation:
The raw IODIR A direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.iodirb
documentation:
The raw IODIR B direction register.  Each bit represents
direction of a pin, either 1 for an input or 0 for an output mode.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gppu
adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gppua
adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.gppub
adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.get_pin
documentation:
Convenience function to create an instance of the DigitalInOut class
pointing at the specified pin of this MCP23S17 device.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.ipol
documentation:
The raw IPOL output register.  Each bit represents the
polarity value of the associated pin (0 = normal, 1 = inverted), assuming that
pin has been configured as an input previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.ipola
documentation:
The raw IPOL A output register.  Each bit represents the
polarity value of the associated pin (0 = normal, 1 = inverted), assuming that
pin has been configured as an input previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.ipolb
documentation:
The raw IPOL B output register.  Each bit represents the
polarity value of the associated pin (0 = normal, 1 = inverted), assuming that
pin has been configured as an input previously.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.interrupt_configuration
documentation:
The raw INTCON interrupt control register. The INTCON register
controls how the associated pin value is compared for the
interrupt-on-change feature. If  a  bit  is  set,  the  corresponding
I/O  pin  is  compared against the associated bit in the DEFVAL
register. If a bit value is clear, the corresponding I/O pin is
compared against the previous value.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.interrupt_enable
documentation:
The raw GPINTEN interrupt control register. The GPINTEN register
controls the interrupt-on-change feature for each pin. If a bit is
set, the corresponding pin is enabled for interrupt-on-change.
The DEFVAL and INTCON registers must also be configured if any pins
are enabled for interrupt-on-change.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.default_value
documentation:
The raw DEFVAL interrupt control register. The default comparison
value is configured in the DEFVAL register. If enabled (via GPINTEN
and INTCON) to compare against the DEFVAL register, an opposite value
on the associated pin will cause an interrupt to occur.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.io_control
documentation:
The raw IOCON configuration register. Bit 1 controls interrupt
polarity (1 = active-high, 0 = active-low). Bit 2 is whether irq pin
is open drain (1 = open drain, 0 = push-pull). Bit 3 is unused.
Bit 4 is whether SDA slew rate is enabled (1 = yes). Bit 5 is if SPI
address pointer auto-increments (1 = no). Bit 6 is whether interrupt
pins are internally connected (1 = yes). Bit 7 is whether registers
are all in one bank (1 = no), this is silently ignored if set to `1`.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.int_flag
documentation:
Returns a list with the pin numbers that caused an interrupt
port A ----> pins 0-7
port B ----> pins 8-15

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.int_flaga
documentation:
Returns a list of pin numbers that caused an interrupt in port A
pins: 0-7

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.int_flagb
documentation:
Returns a list of pin numbers that caused an interrupt in port B
pins: 8-15

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.clear_ints
documentation: Clears interrupts by reading INTCAP.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.clear_inta
documentation: Clears port A interrupts.

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23s17.MCP23S17.clear_intb
documentation: Clears port B interrupts.

Supports MCP23S17 instance on specified SPI bus and optionally
at the specified SPI address.
pylint: disable=too-many-arguments
pylint: disable=too-many-public-methods

modules.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23017
documentation:
`mcp23017`
====================================================

CircuitPython module for the MCP23017 I2C I/O extenders.

* Author(s): Tony DiCola

Implementation Notes
--------------------

**Hardware:**

* `MCP23017 - i2c 16 input/output port expander
  <https://www.adafruit.com/product/732>`_

* `Adafruit MCP23017 I2C GPIO Expander Breakout - STEMMA QT / Qwiic
  <https://www.adafruit.com/product/5346>`_
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2017 Tony DiCola for Adafruit Industries
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2019 Carter Nelson
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

The DEFVAL and INTCON registers must also be configured if any pins

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23017.MCP23017.default_value

methods.name: adafruit_mcp230xx.mcp23017.MCP23017.io_control
documentation:
The raw IOCON configuration register. Bit 1 controls interrupt
polarity (1 = active-high, 0 = active-low). Bit 2 is whether irq pin
is open drain (1 = open drain, 0 = push-pull). Bit 3 is unused.
Bit 4 is whether SDA slew rate is enabled (1 = yes). Bit 5 is if I2C

pins are internally connected (1 = yes). Bit 7 is whether registers
